description: A Grass Valley brewery pouring 'modern and traditional style beers' alongside 'American pub style food,' in their words. Find the taproom at 468 Sutton Way.
Alta Sierra Village Inn offers lodging in the Alta Sierra area of Grass Valley. The inn is listed at 11858 Tammy Way.
Avanguardia Wines pours its bottlings at a tasting room on Mill Street in Grass Valley.
A Nevada City bed and breakfast with 'six contemporary, eco-friendly rooms.' The inn promises 'clean, comfortable accommodations at affordable rates' on West Broad Street.
A fine-wine shop on Commercial Street in Nevada City, featured in MUSE's 'New Gold in Nevada County.'
Cork 49 is a wine spot on Mill Street in Grass Valley. The name says it plainly: corks get pulled here.
An alcohol-free elixir bar in Nevada City serving 'hand-crafted botanical beverages.' Their invitation: 'Sip the Exotic. Live the Extraordinary.'

A craft hard-kombucha maker with a 'family friendly taproom hidden in the hills of Grass Valley,' in their words — craft brews plus arcade games, arts, and food trucks. Their kombucha travels from the Sacramento region to the Bay Area and Reno.
Iron Door Speakeasy is a bar on West Main Street in Grass Valley, styled after the hidden drinking rooms its name recalls.
Nevada City Classic Cafe is a cafe at 216 Broad Street in Nevada City.
Sopa Thai Cuisine serves Thai food on Commercial Street in Nevada City.
A Nevada City bar with '20 beers on tap,' cocktails, and a live calendar of local bands and DJs. The Fern also hosts pub quiz and open-mic comedy nights on Commercial Street.
The Place on South Pine sits at 110 South Pine Street in Nevada City. The name doubles as the directions.
A cannabis dispensary on Idaho Maryland Road in Grass Valley offering 'premium cannabis at Provisions,' in their words. Online orders are 'usually ready in 10 minutes or less.'
An 'Organic Herbal Apothecary' in downtown Nevada City offering 'Natural Medicines For Body and Soul.' Shelves hold teas, tinctures, medicinal mushrooms, salves, and classes in herbalism.
A superette on Commercial Street in Nevada City, featured in MUSE's 'The Culture of Food in Nevada County,' 'Art on the Walls,' and 'Rural is the New Cool.'

A Truckee burger joint calling itself 'premium local and delicious,' with patties running from beef and bison to ahi tuna and veggie. Sweet potato fries, hand-dipped onion rings, and shakes round out the board on Donner Pass Road.
Clarky's BBQ, Smoke & More brings barbecue and smoked fare to Penn Valley Drive in Penn Valley.
Donner Creek Brewing is a brewery on Deerfield Drive in Truckee.
Everwild Wines is a Nevada County winemaker. Location details are still being confirmed for the map.
A Truckee lodge billing itself 'The Best Hotel on Donner Lake.' Loch Leven Lodge calls itself the town's 'favorite place to unwind,' right on Donner Pass Road.
A Truckee restaurant specializing in 'Sourdough Pizza and Tapas,' pairing naturally leavened pies with small plates on Donner Pass Road.
Siam Cuisine serves Thai food on Donner Pass Road in Truckee.
A Truckee candy maker turning out handmade confections and 'Hand-Dipped Belgian Chocolates.' Cases fill with fudges, caramels, brittles, truffles, and caramel apples on Donner Pass Road.
A Penn Valley brewery promising 'Craft Beer, Food & Friends!' Beers go 'From Grain to Glass' on-site, with a food menu and trivia nights alongside.
Wong's Garden is a restaurant on Deerfield Drive in Truckee, with its menu posted online.
'Fresh Local Craft Jerky' sold from an iconic red caboose on Donner Pass Road in Truckee. Beyond fifteen kinds of beef jerky, the cases run to elk, buffalo, venison, salmon, and ahi.
